Tests the strict equality of the negation of an empty string to true.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the logical NOT operator in JavaScript. It evaluates the expression `!("")` and asserts that the result is `true`. The expression `!("")` converts the empty string `""` to a boolean value, which is `false`, and then applies the NOT operator to produce `true`. This test ensures that the logical NOT operator correctly negates the falsy value of an empty string.
